decision_summary: The critical performance issues and user dissatisfaction necessitate an immediate rollback to restore stability.

key_drivers: Crash rate exceeds critical threshold at 5.6%
User sentiment is severely negative with an average rating of 1.89
rationale.feedback_summary: User sentiment is overwhelmingly negative, with 77.1% of feedback indicating dissatisfaction. The primary concerns revolve around crashes and payment issues, leading to a significant increase in support tickets.

what_increases_confidence: Rollback can be executed quickly (within 15 minutes)
Immediate monitoring can assess recovery post-rollback
what_decreases_confidence: User perceptions may not improve immediately post-rollback
Potential for ongoing technical debt from new technology transitions
Risk of further churn if issues are not comprehensively addressed

risk_register.risk: User churn escalation due to dissatisfaction
risk_register.mitigation: Implement rollback and monitor user feedback closely

risk_register.risk: Reputational damage from negative user sentiment
risk_register.mitigation: Communicate transparently with users about issues and resolutions

risk_register.risk: Technical debt from rushed transitions to new technologies
risk_register.mitigation: Conduct thorough root-cause analysis and testing before future launches

action_plan.action: Initiate rollback to v1.9.4 to restore stability
Set up monitoring for crash rates and user feedback post-rollback
Conduct a comprehensive analysis of user feedback and performance metrics

internal: Hold an emergency meeting with product and engineering teams
Develop a rollback plan and communicate it to all stakeholders
Prepare crisis communication strategy for customer support
external: Issue a public acknowledgment of the issues and steps being taken
Communicate directly with affected customers offering refunds or compensation
Utilize social media for updates and to reassure users
